title: Today's Markets Brief: A Calm Start to June
summary: A look at the latest market movements and what they mean.
imageAlt: Image from source article for Dow Jones Futures: Oil Prices Rise As Trump Makes This Iran Move; Nvidia, Tesla Lead 5 Titans Near Buy Points
imageCaption: Dow Jones Futures: Oil Prices Rise As Trump Makes This Iran Move; Nvidia, Tesla Lead 5 Titans Near Buy Points
imageSourceTitle: Dow Jones Futures: Oil Prices Rise As Trump Makes This Iran Move; Nvidia, Tesla Lead 5 Titans Near Buy Points

body.text: I'm Leo Stone, and welcome to your markets brief. I am looking at the latest news signals, and it seems like we're off to a calm start to June. Dow Jones futures are little changed, along with S&P 500 and Nasdaq futures, after a strong close to May with record highs. It's always interesting to see how the market behaves after a big month, and right now, it looks like investors are taking a bit of a breather.

Another item that caught my eye is the stock market's performance at the end of May. The Dow, S&P 500, and Nasdaq all closed with record highs. What's notable here is that this momentum seems to be carrying over into June, at least for now. The question on everyone's mind is how long this trend will continue. Will we see a summer slump, or can the market keep pushing higher?

My quick read: today's market story has that familiar split-screen feeling. There is real excitement around artificial intelligence and chips, but the room still keeps one eye on risk, because markets enjoy a celebration more when they know where the exits are.
